description: 12V DC solenoid valve, normally closed, 1/2" BSP threaded ports. The garden-irrigation default. Opens when energized; closes (and stays closed) when not.
notes: Normally closed (NC) is safer for water — power loss leaves the valve shut. Needs minimum upstream pressure (~20 kPa) to seal reliably; gravity-only systems may not seat well.

description: 6V or 12V miniature solenoid for low-flow water lines. Hose-barb ports for 6 mm tubing.
notes: Works at near-zero head, unlike the full-size BSP solenoids. Good for gravity-fed reservoirs.

description: One-way check valve, plastic, hose-barb ends. Spring + ball seat, opens at ~5 kPa cracking pressure.
use_cases: prevent siphoning back from pump outlet
isolate two pumps feeding a manifold
notes: Mark direction with the arrow on the body. Cheap and quiet but not for high pressure.

description: 12V DC 2-position 5-way pneumatic solenoid valve, 6 mm push-fit ports. Standard for cylinder actuation in DIY pneumatics.
notes: 5/2 means 5 ports (in, two out, two exhaust), 2 positions (energized/de-energized). Pair with a 6 mm push-in muffler on each exhaust to silence.

description: Motorized ball valve — servo arm rotates a 1/2" BSP ball valve open/closed. Slow (~1 s travel) but full-bore flow when open.
common_suppliers: DIY: McMaster ball valve + Hitec/Tower Pro servo
use_cases: high-flow garden valve where solenoid coil-heating is undesired
battery-powered installations (servo only draws when moving)
notes: Servo holds position passively when not moving — much lower idle power than a solenoid. Slower than a solenoid but fully open when on.
